one_liner: Zendesk offers a mature, broad REST API (Ticketing, Help Center, Voice, etc.) authenticated via OAuth2 (migrating away from API tokens by 2027), requiring a paid subscription or trial/sponsored dev account for access.

auth_methods.notes: Recommended/required going forward. Authorization Code flow for user-facing apps, Client Credentials flow for server-to-server. Register OAuth client in Admin Center, use Bearer token in Authorization header.
Legacy API tokens still work but are being fully deprecated: new token creation blocked from Oct 27 2026, all tokens deactivated by April 30 2027.
Historically supported via email/token or email/password combos for Basic auth, but being phased out alongside API tokens.

self_serve_notes: No permanent free tier for API access; requires a paid subscription (plans starting ~$19/mo with rate limits 200-2500 req/min by tier). Developers can get a 14-day free trial, or Marketplace app developers can request a free non-expiring sponsored account for dev/testing.

api_surface.notes: Comprehensive REST API surface covering Ticketing, Help Center, Voice, Users, Organizations, and more. Well-documented with OAuth security guides and rate limit tiers.

main_blocker: None significant; developers need either a paid plan, trial, or sponsored dev account, and must plan migration from API token auth to OAuth2 before April 2027 deprecation deadline.

evidence.supports: API tokens being deprecated, full removal by April 30, 2027, phased rollout details
OAuth2 Authorization Code and Client Credentials flows, Bearer token usage
No free tier; paid plans starting at $19/month required for API access
14-day trial and sponsored developer accounts available for self-serve testing
Rate limits ranging 200-2500 requests/min depending on plan tier
No free API-only agent license offered

flags: auth-transition-in-progress: API token deprecation through April 2027 may require future toolkit updates
no-permanent-free-tier: self-serve access requires trial, sponsored account, or paid subscription
